title: AI Security Digest — April 22, 2026
description: Briefing on the convergence of RAG security and agentic threats, examining FAUDITOR smart contract fuzzing, RAVEN multi-agent RAG frameworks, Visual Inception memory poisoning, and supply chain risks in AI coding agents.

title: AI Security Digest — April 21, 2026
description: Analysis of the agentic supply chain crisis, covering the Anthropic MCP RCE vulnerability, CoT jailbreaks targeting Large Reasoning Models, MATRIX code watermarking, PolicyGapper privacy compliance, and inference-time safety defenses.

title: AI Security Digest — April 20, 2026
description: Briefing on the intersection of legacy vulnerability management and generative AI safety, examining the 263% CVE surge leading to NIST NVD overhaul, AI-assisted exploit discovery, Claude Mythos offensive capabilities, and privacy-preserving local AI agents.

title: AI Security Digest — April 19, 2026
description: AI security briefing on AI-amplified social engineering and legacy vulnerability exploitation, covering cross-tenant helpdesk impersonation attacks, the persistence of unpatched infrastructure, and AI-driven cyber defense automation.

title: This Week in AI Security — April 19, 2026
description: Weekly analysis covering the transition from model-centric to systemic AI security, examining inference provenance (NeuroTrace), lifecycle security (SafeHarness), edge device and SoC security, and the pluralistic alignment evaluation crisis.

title: AI Security Digest — April 18, 2026
description: Briefing on the shift from input-level filtering to execution-aware defense, covering SAFEHARNESS agent lifecycle security, NeuroTrace inference provenance, SoC emulation verification, adversarial audio injection in LALMs, and QuantileMark multi-bit watermarking.

title: AI Security Digest — April 13, 2026
description: This digest analyzes the convergence of traditional infrastructure vulnerabilities and advanced AI threats like 'Sock Puppeting,' demanding holistic security strategies for modern deployments.

title: AI Security Digest — April 12, 2026
description: This digest covers the evolution of AI security threats, focusing on Adversarial Smuggling Attacks (ASA) against MLLMs and the challenges of maintaining sandbox integrity against agentic risks.

title: This Week in AI Security — April 12, 2026
description: The AI security focus is shifting from prompt injection to the systemic vulnerabilities of autonomous agents, specifically targeting the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and tool-use capabilities.

title: AI Security Digest — April 11, 2026
description: The adversarial landscape is shifting from simple prompt injection to complex, multi-stage, and multimodal attacks targeting agentic workflows and knowledge substrates. PIArena offers a new platform for rigorous defense evaluation.

title: AI Security Digest — April 10, 2026
description: This digest analyzes the shift to kinetic AI risks, detailing how JailWAM forces physical control and why prompt filtering fails against modern injection attacks.

title: AI Security Digest — April 07, 2026
description: This digest addresses the shift from prompt injection to structural threats like DDIPE and memory poisoning in autonomous agent ecosystems, emphasizing the need to secure delegation chains.

title: NeuroStrike: Neuron-Level Attacks on Aligned LLMs
description: This paper introduces NeuroStrike, a neuron-level attack framework revealing that safety alignment in LLMs concentrates in fewer than 1% of neurons, enabling both white-box pruning and black-box surrogate-guided jailbreaks with strong cross-model transferability.

title: AI Security Digest — April 06, 2026
description: A summary of recent AI security research, focusing on inference-time safety steering for generative models and neural decompilation for Dart and Swift. It also discusses the challenges in securing AI-driven automation pipelines and legacy infrastructure.

title: AI Security Digest — April 05, 2026
description: An AI security digest highlighting the evolving threat landscape in autonomous agentic ecosystems, focusing on vulnerabilities in LLMs and agentic codebases.

title: This Week in AI Security — April 05, 2026
description: This week's AI security focus shifts from LLM prompt injection to complex vulnerabilities in autonomous, agentic architectures, emphasizing trust, governance, and the 'unsafe agent' phenomenon.

title: AI Security Digest — April 04, 2026
description: This AI Security Digest highlights advancements in defensive AI frameworks and emerging attack surfaces, focusing on probabilistic attack detection and novel neural network architectures.

title: AI Security Digest — April 03, 2026
description: An overview of recent AI security research, focusing on the challenges posed by autonomous systems and the need for dynamic safety testing methods.

title: AI Security Digest — April 02, 2026
description: This AI Security Digest highlights the evolving threat landscape, focusing on the shift from model-centric to system-level vulnerabilities. It introduces EnsembleSHAP and SABLE as advancements in compositional security frameworks, emphasizing the need for structural interpretability and cross-layer analysis in defense mechanisms.

title: AI Security Digest — April 01, 2026
description: A summary of recent AI security research, focusing on adversarial attacks, multimodal large language models, and the need for formal defensive architectures.

title: AI Security Digest — March 31, 2026
description: An AI security digest highlighting critical issues in large language model security, including reasoning safety, hardware-level side-channels, and the emergence of agentic risk.

title: AI Security Digest — March 30, 2026
description: An AI security digest discussing the capability-vulnerability paradox in LLM-based systems and the implications for smart contract security.

title: AI Security Digest — March 29, 2026
description: This week's AI Security Digest highlights the shift from theoretical prompt engineering vulnerabilities to operational risks in autonomous agentic workflows, emphasizing the importance of robust authorization boundaries and deterministic 'kill switches'.

title: AI Security Digest — March 28, 2026
description: OpenAI launches a bug bounty program for AI model abuse and safety risks, expanding the scope to include model evasion, prompt injection, and system prompt leakage.

title: Bridging Models and Agents: Protocol Architectures and Security in MCP & A2A
description: We analyze the architectures and security models of Model Context Protocol (MCP) and Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol, uncovering attack vectors and proposing mitigations for secure multi-agent AI systems.

title: Fool Me If You Can: On the Robustness of Binary Code Similarity Detection Models
description: We introduce asmFooler, a framework that reveals deep learning-based binary code similarity detection models are highly vulnerable to adversarial semantics-preserving transformations at the binary level.

title: LLM-Based Drug Term Detection in Korean Messenger Conversations
description: We propose an LLM-based detection system for identifying unknown drug slang and variant terms in Korean online conversations, achieving 98.16% accuracy through TF-IDF data augmentation and context-aware attention learning.

title: Trends in Attacks and Defenses against Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Systems
description: A comprehensive survey of security vulnerabilities in RAG systems, classifying adversarial attacks by component—data poisoning, retrieval poisoning, and prompt manipulation—and examining emerging defense strategies.

title: Analysis of Watermarking for AI-Generated Text
description: A systematic analysis of LLM text watermarking techniques, defining eight key properties and seven attack methods, while comparing Zero-bit and Multi-bit approaches for identifying and tracing AI-generated text.

title: The Art of English Idioms: Why We Kick Buckets, Break Legs, and Beat Around Bushes
description: A practical guide to English idioms for non-native speakers, exploring their origins, categories, cross-cultural equivalents, and strategies for mastering the expressions that make English beautifully unpredictable.

title: Idioms: Turbo-Charging Neural Decompilation with User-Defined Types
description: An analysis of Idioms, a framework that advances neural decompilation by jointly predicting source code and user-defined type definitions, using neighboring function context from call graphs to achieve up to 205% improvement over baselines on realistic code.

title: Getting Started with Dezoomify Plus: A Practical Intro Guide
description: A beginner-friendly walkthrough of Dezoomify Plus: what it does, how to download your first image, and when to use the advanced dashboard.

title: Visualizing RAG Security: A Deep Dive with RAG-Vis Playground
description: An interactive journey through the fundamentals of Retrieval-Augmented Generation, its security vulnerabilities, and state-of-the-art defense mechanisms.

title: Pickleguard: Defending Python Applications Against Pickle Deserialization Attacks
description: An introduction to Pickleguard, a defense mechanism that detects and prevents malicious pickle payloads through static analysis, opcode inspection, and allowlist-based filtering before deserialization occurs.

title: Pickle Deserialization Attacks: Understanding Python's Silent RCE Vulnerability
description: A comprehensive guide to Python pickle deserialization vulnerabilities, explaining how attackers exploit the __reduce__ method to achieve remote code execution and why 'never unpickle untrusted data' remains critical security advice.

title: MOEVIL: Poisoning Experts to Compromise the Safety of Mixture-of-Experts LLMs
description: An analysis of MOEVIL, a novel attack that poisons individual experts in FrankenMoE systems to bypass safety alignment, achieving up to 79% attack success while maintaining benign task performance through DPO-based poisoning and latent vector manipulation.

title: LLM Red-Teaming: A Survey of Attack Strategies and Defense Mechanisms
description: A comprehensive overview of LLM red-teaming techniques, covering attack strategies from manual prompt engineering to automated jailbreaking methods like GCG, AutoDAN, PAIR, Crescendo, and GOAT, along with defense mechanisms.

title: GASLITE: Poisoning Dense Embedding-based Retrieval Systems
description: An analysis of GASLITE, a novel attack that poisons dense embedding-based retrieval systems by crafting adversarial passages that appear in top-k results for targeted queries, achieving up to 100% success with minimal corpus contamination.

title: Rescuing the Unpoisoned: Efficient Defense against Knowledge Corruption Attacks on RAG Systems
description: RAGDefender is a lightweight, efficient defense mechanism designed to protect Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems from knowledge corruption attacks

title: AgentFuzz: Automatic Detection of Taint-Style Vulnerabilities in LLM-based Agents
description: An analysis of AgentFuzz, a novel fuzzing framework that automatically detects taint-style vulnerabilities in LLM-based agents through LLM-assisted seed generation, feedback-driven scheduling, and sink-guided mutation.

title: An Information Theoretic Approach to Machine Unlearning
description: A novel zero-shot machine unlearning method using information theory and curvature analysis, enabling efficient removal of data influence without requiring access to the retain set.

title: Contrastive Learning for Code Vulnerability Type Classification
description: A comprehensive analysis of hierarchical contrastive learning approaches for classifying code vulnerabilities into CWE types, addressing long-tail distribution, class isolation, and input length limitations.

title: Machine Unlearning for LLMs: Foundations and the AltPO Approach
description: An introduction to machine unlearning in Large Language Models, covering the TOFU benchmark, various unlearning methods (GradDiff, NPO, IdkPO, AltPO), and the challenges of maintaining model utility while forgetting specific knowledge.

title: Membership Inference Attacks on Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Comprehensive Survey
description: A comprehensive analysis of membership inference attacks against RAG systems, examining three state-of-the-art approaches: RAG-MIA, S²MIA, and MBA, along with their defenses and limitations.

title: AMIDES: Adaptive Misuse Detection for SIEM Rule Evasions
description: Understanding SIEM systems, Sigma rules, and how attackers evade detection through simple command-line obfuscation techniques - leading to the need for adaptive misuse detection.

title: Instruction Backdoor Attacks against Customized LLMs
description: A comprehensive analysis of how malicious instructions can be embedded in customized LLMs to create backdoors that activate on specific triggers, without requiring any model fine-tuning.

title: AutoDAN: Generating Stealthy Jailbreak Prompts on Aligned Large Language Models
description: An analysis of AutoDAN, a novel attack that generates semantically meaningful jailbreak prompts using hierarchical genetic algorithms, achieving high attack success rates while evading perplexity-based detection.

title: Teach LLMs to Phish: Stealing Private Information from Language Models
description: An analysis of neural phishing attacks that teach LLMs to memorize and leak private information by inserting benign-appearing poison data during pretraining, achieving up to 90% secret extraction rates.

title: Friends Don't Let Friends Make Bad Graphs: A Data Visualization Guide
description: A comprehensive guide to common data visualization pitfalls and how to avoid them, covering everything from bar plots vs. scatter plots to colorblind-friendly color scales.

title: Foice: Attacking Voice Authentication Systems with a Single Face Image
description: An analysis of Foice, a novel attack that generates synthetic voice recordings from a single face image to bypass voice authentication systems, achieving up to 100% success rate on commercial platforms like WeChat.

title: LINT: On Large Language Models' Resilience to Coercive Interrogation
description: An analysis of LINT, a novel attack that bypasses LLM safety alignment by exploiting top-k token access to extract harmful content without prompt engineering, achieving near-perfect attack success rates.

title: PassBERT: Improving Real-world Password Guessing via Bi-directional Transformers
description: An analysis of PassBERT, a pre-trained BERT-based framework for real-world password guessing attacks including conditional, targeted, and adaptive rule-based password guessing scenarios.
